description: An extensible, LLM-agnostic storytelling engine that allows users to build, extend, and retell interactive stories through structured folders, dynamic memory, character-driven logic, and local/API LLM integration. Supports rollback, NSFW-safe routing, world modeling, and plugin-based extensibility.
goals: Support modular storypack structure (characters, world, items, meta, style, config)
Enable multi-model integration using both local (Ollama) and remote (OpenAI, Groq, Claude) LLMs
Allow story state continuity across interactions with rollback and snapshot support
Support style-guided storytelling and configurable tone via per-story style guides
Enable immersive character memory and voice modeling, unique per character
Support token-efficient prompt construction and fallback routing between models
Expose OpenChronicle functions through CLI, Discord bot, and web UI
Support converter scripts to package/unpack storypacks and build from raw notes
Maintain LLM abstraction so any compliant model can be plugged in
Keep system compatible with low-resource hardware (NAS, Pi, etc.)
achieved: Basic multi-model support with fallback via models.json
Initial character profile loading and role assignment
Core scene loop operational with prompt compilation and character selection
Dockerized runtime for storypacks, logs, and core logic
Alpha logging and rollback script hooks added
CLI bootup script runs story shell in container environment
Copilot-aware context established for iterative development
current: Story memory management and prompt assembly pipeline
Token-aware routing and basic cost-aware fallbacks
Basic LLM plugin loader with ability to call different models
Prototype of rollback via scene snapshots (planned CLI flag)
Pluggable folder loading with optional metadata.yaml handling
planned: Dynamic routing layer based on content tone and safety classification
LLM-agnostic embedding and memory matching for story reference and continuity
Lightweight CLI launcher with selectable stories and rollback options
Support for tagging and filtering scenes, characters, or memories by tone, topic, or trigger
Storypack template generator and best-guess story builder from unstructured folders
NSFW-safe routing rules with user-configurable toggles
OpenLLM introspection layer to monitor token usage, cost, and memory load
Web UI version of the story engine with auth + save system

roadmap.title: ROUTING + CLASSIFICATION SYSTEM
details: Integrate lightweight classifier (e.g. DistilBERT or similar) to flag NSFW, emotional, or tonal shifts in user content
Route flagged content to appropriate model (e.g. uncensored Ollama model vs. OpenAI API)
Maintain tone continuity across model switches by injecting recent character memory and style guide fragments
Define routing behavior per-character and per-scene via `config/storyname/models.json` or `routing_rules.json`
Ensure logs contain traceable routing decisions for debug and user audit
Use model fallback chains with retry logic if a model fails, exceeds tokens, or encounters safety filter
Optionally run classifier in parallel with context builder to minimize latency
Support future override flags (e.g. `/force-nsfw`, `/override-model`) for power users or testing
